concerns.text: CAPE is defined to already normalise for molecular composition, yet the feature set also includes atom‑type count features (composition counts). This creates potential target leakage or redundancy, which can inflate predictive performance and violate construct validity.

Confounder metadata (lattice system, temperature, solvent presence) are recorded (FR‑013) but the plan does not explicitly state that these variables are incorporated into the MLP feature vector during training (FR‑005). Omitting them may leave important sources of variation uncontrolled.

Partial‑correlation analysis (FR‑014) controls for atom‑type composition, yet the same composition features are also used as predictors. This dual role can bias the partial‑correlation estimate and complicates interpretation of whether the model truly captures topology beyond composition.
